platform.name: FastAPI
platform.category: Frameworks, mobile, games, hardware, and creative ecosystems

assumptions: FastAPI is an open-source framework; the First Steps path does not require a FastAPI Cloud or other vendor account.
The developer has Python and can use a terminal and editor.
No FastAPI project or package installation is assumed.

developer_goal: Install FastAPI, create the simplest Hello World API, run the development server, and observe the root JSON response.

first_success.outcome: A locally running FastAPI development server returns the documented Hello World JSON for GET /.

route_selection.rule: Use the tutorial's First Steps page as the shortest complete local API path; treat deployment as optional.

candidates.name: First Steps local Hello World
candidates.reason: Explicit Check it section ends at the root JSON response before interactive docs and optional deploy.

candidates.name: Interactive API docs at /docs as first success
candidates.reason: Documented only after the root JSON response already proves the API works.

candidates.name: FastAPI Cloud deployment
candidates.reason: Marked optional and placed after local first success.

required_path.action: Create and activate a project virtual environment, then install fastapi[standard].
required_path.observable_result: The FastAPI package and standard extras, including the CLI, are installed in the active environment.

required_path.action: Create main.py with the official simplest FastAPI app that returns {"message": "Hello World"} on GET /.
required_path.observable_result: main.py defines app and the root path operation.

required_path.action: From the project directory, run the FastAPI development server (fastapi dev).

required_fields.label: fastapi dev
required_fields.notes: Current First Steps examples also show uv run fastapi dev; the tutorial install path uses the standard package CLI.

required_path.observable_result: Uvicorn reports the app is running at http://127.0.0.1:8000.

required_path.action: Open http://127.0.0.1:8000 and inspect the JSON response.

prerequisites.description: Python installed with ability to create a virtual environment and install packages.
A terminal and an editor to create main.py.

unavoidable_waits.description: Package download and application startup before Uvicorn listens on port 8000.

platform_outcomes.description: The FastAPI CLI starts the development server and serves the root path operation.

excluded.reason: Documented after the Check it root JSON response.
Marked optional and after local success.

excluded.item: Reading the full tutorial beyond First Steps
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a product action.

sources.title: Virtual Environments
evidence_supported: Create and activate a project virtual environment before install

uncertainties.question: How should the corpus account-creation starting boundary map for FastAPI when First Steps needs no vendor account?
uncertainties.impact: Blocks verified status under the account-creation contract despite a well-documented local Hello World path.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Corpus policy for open-source frameworks without platform accounts.
